name: Chocolate Mousse
recipeInstructions:
- Crust
1. Mix the first three ingredients and press into the bottom of a 9-1/2 inch spring-form pan.
2. Bake at 350 degrees for 8 minutes.
- Bottom Layer
1. Melt the chocolate in the top of a double boiler, cool until lukewarm.
2. Beat the egg yolks, adding the sugar a little at a time until pale yellow and it forms a ribbon.
3. Blend in liqueur, then melted chocolate.
4. Beat chilled whipping cream to soft peaks. Fold in 1/3 cup of the whipped cream, then the remaining.
5. Pour the mixture into the pan, smooth the top, and freeze until set for about 30 minutes.
- Top Layer
1. Bring 3/4 cup of cream to a boil, reduce heat, and cool for 2 minutes.
2. Finely chop white chocolate in a food processor and pour hot cream through the feed tube, blending until smooth. Transfer to a medium bowl, cool completely. Stir liqueur into white chocolate.
3. Beat remaining whipping cream to soft peaks. Fold in 1/3 of the cream, then fold in the remaining.
4. Pour over the bottom layer, smooth the top, and freeze until set for at least 24 hours. Run a knife around the edge, remove the ring, return to freezer.
- Decoration
1. Melt chocolate and butter in a heavy pan, cool to lukewarm.
2. Pipe decorations and freeze until set for about 10 minutes.
NOTE: Can be prepared up to 2 days ahead, covered with plastic wrap and frozen. Frozen mousse can be cut into wedges to serve.
